*Content was generated by Bard*

Google Cloud Compute Engine (GCE) is a cloud computing service that offers virtual machines (VMs) on demand. GCE VMs can be used for a variety of workloads, including web serving, data processing, and machine learning.

GPUs (graphics processing units) are specialized processors that are designed to accelerate graphics and video processing. GPUs can be used to improve the performance of GCE VMs that are running graphics-intensive workloads.

To use GPUs with GCE, you can create a VM instance with a GPU attached. You can then install the necessary drivers and software to use the GPU.

Here are some of the benefits of using GPUs with GCE:

* Improved performance for graphics-intensive workloads
* Reduced costs for running graphics-intensive workloads
* Increased flexibility for running graphics-intensive workloads

If you are running graphics-intensive workloads on GCE, then you should consider using GPUs to improve performance and reduce costs.

Here are some of the things you can do with GPUs on GCE:

* Train machine learning models
* Render 3D graphics
* Process video
* Encode and decode video
* Stream games

GPUs are a powerful tool that can be used to improve the performance of a wide variety of workloads. If you are running graphics-intensive workloads on GCE, then you should consider using GPUs to improve performance and reduce costs.

### Deploying ML APIs using GKE

1. Pre-requisites
2. Reserving Static IP
3. Creating Compute Engine instances with GPUs
4. Connecting to the Instance via SSH
5. Configuring NVIDIA driver
6. Starting vLLM Docker Image
7. Testing API methods
8. Starting h2oGPT Docker Image

#### Pre-requisites

- Acccess to GCP with a proper billing account along with Compute Engine and GPU devices
- Service Accounts and Network settings must have been configured properly
- Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud` and `gsutil`) must have been installed and configured
- The following environment variables must have been set

```shell
PROJECT_ID=<GCP Project ID>
REGION=<Region>
ZONE=<Zone>
SERVICE_ACCOUNT=<Service Account>
SUBNET=<Subnet>
```

#### Reserving Static IP

```shell
gcloud compute addresses create h2o-gpt-ip --region $REGION
gcloud compute addresses describe h2o-gpt-ip --region $REGION
```

- Replace the following IP Address

```shell
IP_ADDRESS=[IP_ADDRESS]
```

#### Creating Compute Engine instances with GPUs

```shell
gcloud compute instances create h2o-gpt \
    --project=$PROJECT_ID \
    --zone=$ZONE \
    --machine-type=n1-standard-16 \
    --network-interface=address=$IP_ADDRESS,network-tier=PREMIUM,subnet=$SUBNET \
    --maintenance-policy=TERMINATE \
    --provisioning-model=STANDARD \
    --service-account=$SERVICE_ACCOUNT \
    --scopes=https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform \
    --accelerator=count=4,type=nvidia-tesla-t4-vws \
    --tags=allow-8000,http-server \
    --create-disk=auto-delete=yes,boot=yes,device-name=h2o-gpt-disk,image=projects/ml-images/global/images/c2-deeplearning-pytorch-2-0-gpu-v20230615-debian-11-py310,mode=rw,size=250,type=projects/$PROJECT_ID/zones/$ZONE/diskTypes/pd-balanced \
    --no-shielded-secure-boot \
    --shielded-vtpm \
    --shielded-integrity-monitoring \
    --labels=goog-ec-src=vm_add-gcloud \
    --reservation-affinity=any \
    --metadata=google-logging-enabled=true
```

#### Connecting to the Instance via SSH

```shell
gcloud compute start-iap-tunnel h2o-gpt 22 --local-host-port=localhost:6000 --zone $ZONE --project $PROJECT_ID
```

```shell
gcloud compute ssh h2o-gpt --project $PROJECT_ID --zone $ZONE --tunnel-through-iap
```

#### Configuring NVIDIA driver

```shell
sudo su -
```

```shell
nvidia-smi
```

```shell
sudo /opt/deeplearning/install-driver.sh
```

```shell
sudo apt-get update \
    && sudo apt-get install -y nvidia-container-toolkit-base
sudo nvidia-ctk runtime configure --runtime=docker
sudo systemctl restart docker
```

#### Starting vLLM Docker Image

```shell
sudo su

unset CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
mkdir -p $HOME/.cache/huggingface/hub
mkdir -p $HOME/save
docker run \
    --runtime=nvidia \
    --gpus '"device=0,1"' \
    --shm-size=10.24gb \
    -p 8000:5000 \
    --init -d \
    --entrypoint /h2ogpt_conda/vllm_env/bin/python3.10 \
    -e NCCL_IGNORE_DISABLED_P2P=1 \
    -v /etc/passwd:/etc/passwd:ro \
    -v /etc/group:/etc/group:ro \
    -u `id -u`:`id -g` \
    -v "${HOME}"/.cache:/workspace/.cache \
    gcr.io/vorvan/h2oai/h2ogpt-runtime:0.1.0 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
        --port=5000 \
        --host=0.0.0.0 \
        --model=h2oai/h2ogpt-4096-llama2-7b-chat \
        --tokenizer=hf-internal-testing/llama-tokenizer \
        --tensor-parallel-size=2 \
        --seed 1234 \
        --trust-remote-code \
        --download-dir=/workspace/.cache/huggingface/hub
        # h2oai/h2ogpt-gm-oasst1-en-2048-falcon-7b-v3
```

> In order to activate the chat completion Install fschat as below Restart the container

```shell
docker exec -it <container_id> bash
/h2ogpt_conda/vllm_env/bin/pip3.10 install fschat
docker stop <container_id>
docker start <container_id>
```

#### Testing API methods

*(Do not forget to replace IP_ADDRESS)*

```shell
curl http://[IP_ADDRESS]:8000/v1/completions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
    "model": "h2oai/h2ogpt-4096-llama2-7b-chat",
    "prompt": "San Francisco is a",
    "max_tokens": 7,
    "temperature": 0
    }'
```

*(Do not forget to replace IP_ADDRESS)*

```python
from langchain.llms import VLLMOpenAI

llm = VLLMOpenAI(
    openai_api_key="EMPTY",
    openai_api_base="http://[IP_ADDRESS]:8000/v1",
    model_name="h2oai/h2ogpt-4096-llama2-7b-chat",
    model_kwargs={"stop": ["."]}
)
print(llm("Rome is"))
```

*(Do not forget to replace IP_ADDRESS)*

```shell
curl -X 'POST'   \
  'http://[IP_ADDRESS]:8000/v1/chat/completions'   \
  -H 'accept: application/json'   \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
  "model": "h2oai/h2ogpt-4096-llama2-7b-chat",
  "messages": [
      {
        "role": "system",
        "content": ""
      },
      {
        "role": "user",
        "content": "Hello!"
      }
    ]
}'
```

*(Do not forget to replace IP_ADDRESS)*

```python
import openai
# Modify OpenAI's API key and API base to use vLLM's API server.
openai.api_key = "EMPTY"
openai.api_base = "http://[IP_ADDRESS]:8000/v1"

response = openai.ChatCompletion.create(
    model="h2oai/h2ogpt-4096-llama2-7b-chat",
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Who won the world series in 2020?"},
        {"role": "assistant", "content": "The Los Angeles Dodgers won the World Series in 2020."},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Where was it played?"}
    ]
)

print(response)
```

#### Starting h2oGPT Docker Image

*(Do not forget to replace IP_ADDRESS)*

```shell
mkdir -p ~/.cache
mkdir -p ~/save
docker run \
    --gpus '"device=2,3"' \
    --runtime=nvidia \
    --shm-size=2g \
    -p 80:7860 \
    --init -d \
    -v /etc/passwd:/etc/passwd:ro \
    -v /etc/group:/etc/group:ro \
    -u `id -u`:`id -g` \
    -v "${HOME}"/.cache:/workspace/.cache \
    -v "${HOME}"/save:/workspace/save \
    gcr.io/vorvan/h2oai/h2ogpt-runtime:0.1.0 /workspace/generate.py \
        --inference_server="vllm:[IP_ADDRESS]:8000" \
        --base_model=h2oai/h2ogpt-4096-llama2-7b-chat \
        --langchain_mode=UserData
```
